articles.title: Maya Jama Wants to Be a Villain in Movies
articles.summary: Maya Jama is a famous TV host from Britain who presents shows like Love Island and The Masked Singer. She is 31 years old and lives in Manchester with her partner, a football player. When she was younger, Maya dreamed of being an actor but got many rejections and felt sad, so she stopped trying. Instead, she started making videos on YouTube and later became a TV presenter. Now, she is getting a new chance to act and will appear in a Netflix series called The Gentlemen. Maya says she is ready to try something different and wants to play a monster or villain in movies, not a nice character like herself. She thinks it would be fun to be the opposite of what people expect.

articles.title: Water Helps Diesel Engines Pollute Less
articles.summary: Scientists in Nigeria found a cool way to make diesel engines cleaner. Diesel engines are used in trucks, tractors, and machines because they are strong, but they also make dirty air that can make people sick. The new idea is called Water-in-Diesel Emulsion, or WiDE for short. It mixes tiny drops of water into diesel fuel with special chemicals. When the fuel burns in the engine, the water turns to steam and helps the fuel burn better. This means less harmful stuff like nitrogen oxides and soot comes out of the exhaust. Studies show it can cut pollution by over 60% without hurting how well the engine works. It might even make engines use fuel more efficiently. This is good news because it's a simple and cheap way to clean up diesel engines without changing them.

articles.title: Judge Stops Investigation into Federal Reserve
articles.summary: A judge in the United States has stopped the Department of Justice from investigating the Federal Reserve, which is the country's central bank. The judge said there was no evidence to support the investigation. The Federal Reserve chairman, Jerome Powell, is happy because he thought the investigation was just a way to pressure the bank to lower interest rates. The prosecutor, Jeanine Pirro, wants to appeal the decision and says it is wrong. This case is about political pressure on the bank and makes it harder for the White House to choose a new chairman. The prosecutor is an ally of former President Donald Trump and issued subpoenas to get information about office renovations. Powell accused the Trump administration of using the probe to pressure the bank. The judge ruled that the subpoenas were for an improper purpose to harass Powell. Some senators do not think more investigation is needed.

articles.title: Harry Styles Sets New Record with His Album
articles.summary: Harry Styles is a famous singer who just released a new album called Kiss All The Time. Disco Occasionally. It became number one in the UK album charts, which means it sold the most copies in its first week. He sold over 183,000 copies, which is more than his last album. Many people bought it on vinyl records, making it the biggest physical album of the year. Some critics did not love the album, saying it was nice but not deep, while others liked it for being happy and personal. Harry said he made the album after touring for a long time and wanted to be more open to new experiences. He even had a special concert in Manchester that was shown on Netflix.

articles.title: US Checks Other Countries for Unfair Work Practices
articles.summary: The United States is starting a new investigation to look at 60 countries, including China, the European Union, Canada, and the UK. They want to see if these countries are allowing goods made with forced labor to be sold, which might hurt American businesses. Forced labor means people are made to work against their will, often in bad conditions. The US says this gives foreign companies an unfair advantage because they can make things cheaper. This investigation could lead to new taxes called tariffs on goods from these countries. The US has done similar checks before, like with China, and has laws to stop goods from places where forced labor happens. Public hearings will happen in April to talk about this issue.

articles.title: A Man Who Stood Up to Putin and Became Famous
articles.summary: Pavel Talankin, also called Pasha, was a school worker in Russia who made a film about standing up to President Putin. He had to leave his home for safety and moved to another country. His film, made with a friend, won awards and is now up for an Oscar. Pasha uses humor in his film to talk about serious things like war. He met famous actors in Hollywood and is excited about the Oscar award. The story shows how one person can become a hero by speaking out.
